stages.label: Source Identified
stages.description: A source relevant to a software supply-chain control has been identified.
stages.minimum_support_expected: A source record with a stable locator such as a repository URL, release page, advisory, policy page, build log, or attestation.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Treating the existence of a source as evidence that the control is operating effectively.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Extract a bounded finding tied to one control and one reviewed artifact or observation.

stages.label: Observation Extracted
stages.description: A control-relevant observation has been extracted from a source.
stages.minimum_support_expected: A finding that records the reviewed component, source locator, observed control signal, and boundary.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Converting one observation into a broad assurance claim.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Map the observation into a support map that explains what it does and does not support.

stages.label: Claim Supported
stages.description: A public control claim is backed by linked findings and sources.
stages.minimum_support_expected: A claim with supporting evidence entries, cited findings, cited sources, and explicit limitations.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Implying security assurance beyond the reviewed release, dependency, or time window.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Independent review accepts source fidelity, control fit, risk framing, and operational boundary.

stages.label: Reviewed Control Baseline
stages.description: Required appraisal lanes have accepted the control baseline for publication.
stages.minimum_support_expected: Complete non-blocking evidence appraisals for all default lanes.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Treating a reviewed baseline as permanent assurance rather than a time-bounded assessment.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Review Update refreshes stale control coverage or stages a material update.
